skill.name: workflow-orchestration-patterns
skill.description: Design durable workflows with Temporal for distributed systems. Covers workflow vs activity separation, saga patterns, state management, and determinism constraints. Use when building long-running processes, distributed transactions, or microservice orchestration.
skill.summary: Design Temporal workflow architectures for reliable distributed processes.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external command, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found Markdown guidance only; the alerts are false positives from backticks, code fences, headings, and workflow terminology, with no executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection, or data exfiltration evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as External Commands
low_findings.description: The detected locations are Markdown backticks, code fences, and inline examples. They do not instruct the assistant to run shell commands, and no executable script is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains documentation text and Markdown formatting only. I found no shell syntax, command invocation, script file, or user-input execution path at these locations.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Alerts Match Documentation Text
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts do not identify cryptographic code or algorithms. The referenced lines describe Temporal concepts, headings, links, and workflow design guidance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no cryptographic implementation, hash function, cipher, or security-sensitive algorithm selection. The alerts appear to be token matches in prose.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Alert on Validation Text
low_findings.description: The flagged line is a workflow error-handling example for invalid input. It does not request host inspection, environment discovery, or collection of system details.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding section discusses non-retryable workflow errors. I found no commands such as uname, whoami, env, ps, or filesystem enumeration.

content.value_statement: Long-running distributed processes are hard to design and recover safely. This skill gives practical Temporal workflow patterns for orchestration, retries, compensation, and state management.
actual_capabilities: Explains when Temporal workflow orchestration is appropriate.
Separates workflow responsibilities from activity responsibilities.
Describes saga compensation for distributed transactions.
Covers entity workflows, fan-out fan-in, and async callback patterns.
Lists determinism constraints for workflow code.
Summarizes retry policies, idempotency, heartbeats, and scaling practices.
limitations: It provides architecture guidance, not runnable implementation code.
It focuses on Temporal concepts and may not fit other orchestration engines.
It does not inspect a live Temporal cluster or deployment.
The referenced implementation playbook file is not included in this package.

use_cases.title: Design an order workflow
use_cases.description: Plan inventory reservation, payment, fulfillment, compensation, and retry behavior for an order process.

use_cases.title: Review workflow boundaries
use_cases.description: Check whether business logic belongs in a workflow or activity before implementation.

use_cases.title: Improve operational resilience
use_cases.description: Apply heartbeats, timeouts, idempotency, and child workflow decomposition to existing Temporal designs.

prompt_templates.title: Classify workflow steps
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the workflow orchestration patterns skill to classify these process steps as workflows or activities: [paste steps]. Explain each boundary.

prompt_templates.title: Design a saga
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the workflow orchestration patterns skill to design a saga for [business process]. Include forward steps, compensations, and failure behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Review determinism risks
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the workflow orchestration patterns skill to review this Temporal workflow design for determinism risks: [paste design]. List required changes.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a resilient architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the workflow orchestration patterns skill to design a resilient Temporal architecture for [system]. Cover child workflows, retries, idempotency, signals, and monitoring.

output_examples.input: Classify an order checkout flow with inventory, payment, and fulfillment steps.
output_examples.output: The response separates orchestration decisions from external activity calls, then identifies compensations for inventory release, payment refund, and fulfillment cancellation.

output_examples.input: Review a workflow that calls a payment API directly and uses system time.
output_examples.output: The response flags direct API calls and non-deterministic time use, then recommends activities and deterministic workflow time APIs.

output_examples.input: Plan scaling for one million independent work items.
output_examples.output: The response recommends bounded parent workflows, child workflow decomposition, task queue planning, and monitoring for retry and backlog behavior.

best_practices: Keep orchestration decisions in workflows and external effects in activities.
Register compensation before each saga step executes.
Make activities idempotent and configure timeouts for every external operation.
anti_patterns: Calling external APIs directly from workflow code.
Using non-deterministic time, random values, or global state inside workflows.
Building large unbounded workflows instead of decomposing work into child workflows.

faq.question: What is this skill for?
faq.answer: It helps design durable Temporal workflows for distributed systems and long-running business processes.

faq.question: Does it generate production code?
faq.answer: No. It provides design guidance, review checklists, and architecture patterns.

faq.question: Which orchestration platform does it target?
faq.answer: It focuses on Temporal workflow orchestration concepts and terminology.

faq.question: Can it help with saga design?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains forward steps, compensation order, idempotency, and failure handling.

faq.question: Does it cover operational concerns?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes retries, heartbeats, timeout planning, monitoring signals, and scaling guidance.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. Manual review found only documentation content and false-positive static alerts.
